steps.title: Find the equation of the shown line
steps.content: The graph shown is $y=f(x)+19$. Two clear points on the line are $(-1,7)$ and $(0,3)$, so the slope is $$\frac{3-7}{0-(-1)}=-4.$$ Since the line crosses the $y$-axis at $3$, the shown line is $y=-4x+3$.

steps.title: Undo the vertical shift
steps.content: Because the shown graph is $y=f(x)+19$, subtract $19$ from the equation of the shown line: $$f(x)+19=-4x+3$$ $$f(x)=-4x-16$$

steps.title: Match the signs
steps.content: The function $f(x)=-4x-16$ has a negative slope and a negative y-intercept. With $c\gt 0$ and $d\gt 0$, the form $-d-cx$ also has a negative y-intercept and a negative slope.

steps.title: Match the form
steps.content: The matching choice, $f(x)=-d-cx$, works with $d=16$ and $c=4$. The other choices have a positive intercept, a positive slope, or both. A is correct.
